name: Print all nodes at a distance of K in BT
category: Binary Trees [Traversals, Medium and Hard Problems]
statement:
Given the root of a binary tree, the value of a target node target, and an integer k. Return an array of the values of all nodes that have a distance k from the target node.


The answer can be returned in any order (N represents null).


Note: Although input shows target as a value, internally it refers to the TreeNode with that value.
constraints:
• 1 <= Number of Nodes <= 103
  • -104 <= Node.val <= 104
  • All the values Node.val are unique.
  • target is the value of one of the nodes in the tree
  • 0 <= k <= 103

examples.explanation: The nodes that are a distance 2 from the target node (with value 5) have values 7, 4, and 1.
The nodes that are a distance 3 from the target node (with value 5) have values 0, 8.
